DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-25-2021 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS YEONG WO ASSOCIATION, KWOK LEUNG'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

No appearances are made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Co-defendant Leung's dilatory demurrer is overruled. The complaint is not uncertain and it properly states a cause of action against Leung because he is an indispensable party. The Court liberally construes the complaint pursuant to Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 452. Plaintiff seeks to have the resolution confirming Leung as President of the Yeong Wo Association invalidated, which would cause Leung to lose his position as President of that organization. Leung is therefore properly named in this action. (See Washington Mutual Bank v. Blechman (2007) 157 Cal.App.4th 662, 667 ["A person is an indispensable party to litigation if his or her rights must necessarily be affected by the judgment. Stated differently, [w]here the plaintiff seeks some type of affirmative relief which, if granted, would injure or affect the interest of a third person not joined, that third person is an indispensable party." (citations and quotations omitted)].)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)
